content.problemStatement:
Our Chef is catering for a big corporate office party and is busy preparing different mouth watering dishes. The host has insisted that he serves his delicious cupcakes for dessert. 

 On the day of the party, the Chef was over-seeing all the food arrangements as well, ensuring that every item was in its designated position. The host was satisfied with everything except the cupcakes. He noticed they were arranged neatly in the shape of a rectangle. He asks the Chef to make it as square-like as possible. 

 The Chef is in no mood to waste his cupcakes by transforming it into a perfect square arrangement. Instead, to fool the host, he asks you to arrange the **N** cupcakes as a rectangle so that the **difference** between the length and the width is minimized.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the number of cupcakes.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum possible difference between the length and the width of a rectangular arrangement of N cupcakes.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=20 the divisor closest to sqrt(20) is 4, giving 4×5 and diff 1. For N=13 (prime) only 1×13 exists, diff 12. For N=8 the best pair is 2×4, diff 2. For N=4 we can use 2×2, diff 0.
N=1 gives 1×1 diff 0. N=100000000 is a perfect square (10000×10000) diff 0.

solution.approach: For a given N we need two integers a and b such that a * b = N, a <= b and (b - a) is minimal. The optimal a is the largest divisor of N that does not exceed sqrt(N). Starting from floor(sqrt(N)) and moving downwards we find the first i that divides N; then a = i and b = N / i. The answer is b - a. This works because any divisor larger than sqrt(N) would produce a smaller counterpart below sqrt(N). The algorithm runs in O(sqrt(N)) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * sqrt(N)) – for each test case we scan downwards from sqrt(N) until a divisor is found.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
